description: Diagnose the seeded electronics-spike anomaly. Input is the Anomaly object produced by seededToAnomaly(electronics-spike-w2) — same shape the production monitoring agent emits.

anomaly.impact: order_count for category:electronics rose ~150% in a discrete window. Investigate the root cause.

golden_output.conclusion: The electronics order_count spike (+14,056% vs 12-week baseline, from ~4 orders/week to 588 in week of Dec 8) is part of a broad, platform-wide pre-Christmas demand surge that began sharply on 2025-12-08. Every category, state, and payment type rose simultaneously in that week — electronics simply experienced the most dramatic absolute and relative lift, likely because high-value electronics are disproportionately purchased as holiday gifts. No isolated electronics-specific promotion, regional event, or payment method change drove the spike.
evidence: Electronics order_count jumped from a 12-week baseline avg of 4.15 orders/week to 588 in week of 2025-12-08, a +14,056% change (141×).

All other categories rose in the same week: home_decor +2,129%, toys +1,884%, fashion +1,762%, sports +1,653%, health_beauty +1,601%, food_drink +1,454% — confirming a platform-wide lift, not an electronics-specific event.
All 26 Brazilian states saw order_count increases in the anomaly week: ES +6,660%, TO +6,400%, PA +5,620%, SP +3,316%, with no state declining — ruling out a regional concentration hypothesis.
All payment types rose proportionally: boleto +3,309%, credit_card +3,019%, voucher +2,933%, debit_card +2,500% — ruling out a payment-method-driven change.

Weekly time series shows orders at 54 in week of 2025-12-01, then jumping to 588 in week of 2025-12-08 — a sudden single-week cliff consistent with an external seasonal trigger (Black Friday / pre-Christmas), not a gradual trend.

Sample orders from 2025-12-08 are all 'delivered' or 'shipped' with valid electronics items (prices ranging R$210 to R$2,041), confirming real fulfilled transactions rather than a data ingestion artifact.

hypothesesConsidered.hypothesis: Broad platform-wide pre-Christmas/holiday seasonal demand surge starting Dec 8
hypothesesConsidered.reasoning: Every category (6/6 checked), every state (26/26), and every payment type (4/4) rose sharply in the same week. The time series shows a sudden step-change on 2025-12-08 — the start of Brazil's peak holiday shopping window. Electronics, being a popular high-value gift category, led the surge in relative terms but was not isolated from broader demand acceleration.

hypothesesConsidered.hypothesis: Regional concentration — a specific state (e.g. SP) drove the electronics spike
hypothesesConsidered.reasoning: SP rose 33× but so did every other state, with smaller states like ES (+66.6×) and TO (+64×) growing even faster. No single region is the cause; the surge is geographically uniform across all 26 states.

hypothesesConsidered.hypothesis: Payment method shift — a new or promotional payment option (e.g. vouchers) drove electronics purchases
hypothesesConsidered.reasoning: All four payment types (credit_card, boleto, voucher, debit_card) rose in near-identical proportions (25×–33×). There is no payment-type that spiked disproportionately, ruling out a payment-driven mechanism.

affectedCustomers.segmentDescription: Orders in the electronics category during the week of 2025-12-08 across all Brazilian states and payment types; up from a baseline of ~4 orders/week

scoring_config.notes: PR E established this anomaly scores 6/10 (60%) on the diagnostic rubric — middle tier. Regression check anchors on the supported hypothesis.
